title: ‘Real Housewives of Rhode Island’ Episode 4: Red pizza, rumors, and railroad rides

title: Amtrak releases first look at new long distance fleet
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: New England Travel The new rail cars will "bring travelers the world-class service they deserve.
" Amtrak Amtrak A new era in American train travel is coming, Amtrak says.
The company announced on Wednesday that it has issued a formal request for suppliers to bid on its new long-distance fleet replacement contract.

title: Clarke’s, a 50-year-old bar at Faneuil Hall, has closed
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Food News The owners told The Boston Globe that foot traffic downtown had not bounced back since the pandemic.
Clarke's has closed after 50 years in business.
Jonathan Wiggs/Globe Staff ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: With the Boston Marathon finish line in sight, they sacrificed personal goals to help a runner in need
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Boston Marathon Israel Rivera and Henco Visser embodied the spirit of the 2026 Boston Marathon when they helped Jonathan Adams cross the finish line on Monday.
Israel Rivera (L) and Henco Visser (R) carry Jonathan Adams along Boylston Street at the 2026 Boston Marathon.
Autumn Sloboda / Boston.

bullets: Local News "Increasing clouds tonight with showers arriving from west to east early Wednesday morning.

National Weather Service: ‘Increasing clouds tonight with showers arriving from west to east early Wednesday morning’“Mostly sunny and dry, but cool to go along with seabreezes near the coasts.

title: Former Brigham and Women’s doctor indicted on 81 new sexual assault charges
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Crime Dr.
Derrick Todd is accused of performing unnecessary breast and pelvic exams on patients.
He now faces more than 100 sexual assault charges in all.

title: The ‘Mother of the Boston Tea Party’ honored with new Medford statue
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News A long-overlooked American Revolutionary figure is recognized on Patriots' Day.
A Medford woman credited with playing a key role in the Boston Tea Party was honored Monday with a statue unveiled outside Medford City Hall.

Sarah Bradlee Fulton, often referred to as the “Mother of the Boston Tea Party,” helped disguise participants by painting their faces and concealing their clothing, according to the Boston Tea Party Ships and Museum.

title: Review and setlist: Florence + The Machine delivered a dark, intimate Boston show

bullets: Concert Reviews Florence + The Machine’s rescheduled Boston stop traded arena spectacle for bare feet, huge vocals, and striking intimacy.
Florence Welch of Florence + The Machine.

title: Mike Vrabel issues new statement on Dianna Russini photos, acknowledges ‘difficult conversations’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: New England Patriots "We believe in order to be successful on and off the field, we have to make good decisions.
That includes me.
That starts with me.

title: Couple runs the Boston Marathon in wedding attire
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston Marathon “The marathon has always been a very strong part of our relationship.
” Paulina Santoyo (left) and Ryan Foreman (right) pose at the Boston Marathon.
Courtesy of Paulina Santoyo The Boston Marathon is known for its sea of colorful racing gear — but one couple still managed to stand out.
